# The Faulkner Group

## What We Do
The Faulkner Group is a boutique healthcare advisory firm that serves as the strategic clinical and operational partner for women's health tech companies. We bridge the gap between innovative health technology platforms and real-world clinical adoption. We embed directly inside organizations to drive physician adoption, build clinical governance frameworks, and convert stalled pilots into enterprise-wide deployments.

## Who We Serve
- Women's health tech founders and CEOs who have closed a pilot but are stuck scaling due to physician resistance, missing governance documentation, or payer hesitation.
- Venture capital and private equity firms with women's health portfolio companies that are stalling post-investment and need clinical and operational diligence before a Series B, add-on acquisition, or board review.

## Our Founders
- John Faulkner, CEO and Healthcare IT Architect. 15+ years designing and optimizing healthcare IT infrastructure and EHR systems nationwide. Former Marine Corps Officer Candidate trained at Quantico, VA. Specializes in turning stalled implementations into scalable, working solutions. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/johnathonfaulkner/
- Dr. Nicole Faulkner, Chief Clinical Officer. Board-certified OB/GYN, Medical Director at BackTable (physician-to-physician podcast network), featured speaker at the Women's Health Innovation Summit (WHIS). Brings clinical credibility and a physician network that opens doors no sales deck can. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicole-faulkner-d-o-1136a4370/

## The 4 Pillars of Clinical Adoption Framework
1. Clinical Readiness and Governance: Environment audits, integration barrier identification, governance protocols, CMIO engagement strategies, and risk frameworks for health systems and payers.
2. Physician Adoption and Champion Development: Peer-to-peer advocacy, KOL engagement, internal champion development that drives adoption from the inside out.
3. Training, Education, and Change Management: Structured training programs from front-desk staff to attending physicians. No shelfware. No workarounds.
4. Sales, Customer Success, and Deployment: Clinical credibility in the sales process, full pilot-to-enterprise deployment lifecycle with structured support at every stage.

The Faulkner Group offers a Clinical Clarity Session to assess which pillars are most at risk for your specific product.

## Key Proof Point
90% of health tech pilots fail to convert to full-scale implementation without clinical alignment (Accenture Health, Digital Health Scaling Report). The Faulkner Group guarantees to deliver a clinical adoption strategy that accelerates physician buy-in, or they keep working until they do.

## Services
- Sprint Starter: Clarity in 48 hours. Focused diagnostic across product workflows, go-to-market operations, and clinical positioning. Includes a 90-minute strategy session with both co-founders.
- Sprint Pro: Build. Optimize. Operate. EHR integration, clinical workflow design, and physician adoption strategy with clinical collaboration from a board-certified OB/GYN advisor and weekly advisory sessions.
- Sprint Elite: End-to-end enterprise engagement from strategy through launch. Deep EHR integration expertise, AI readiness assessment, and intelligent workflow integration for women's health technology platforms.
- Partnership Model: Long-term embedded advisory for women's health tech companies. Includes ongoing clinical advisory, fractional Chief Clinical Systems Officer support, strategic guidance, and access to OB/GYN, operator, and health tech leader network.

## Clinical Clarity Session — Tier-1 Offer
- Price: $1,500
- Format: 90 minutes, virtual
- Includes: Pre-session product review, live diagnostic with a board-certified OB/GYN and Healthcare IT Architect, identification of the 1–3 highest-risk failure points, prioritized next steps, and a written Clinical Clarity Summary delivered within 48 hours.
- Best for: Founders in active health system conversations, approaching a pilot, preparing for investor due diligence, or recovering from a pilot that didn't go as planned.
- Booking: https://calendly.com/d/cx9r-9x8-gbf/clinical-clarity-session

## Free Interactive Tools
- Reimbursement Readiness Scorecard: 10-question assessment across product readiness, payer strategy, and market access. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/reimbursement-scorecard
- Hospital Partnership Fit Checker: 6-question assessment to determine which type of health system partnership fits your stage. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/hospital-fit-checker
- Femtech Funding Gap Benchmarker: 6-question tool to benchmark your raise against femtech peers by stage and team composition. URL: https://thrive-beacon-studio.lovable.app/for-womens-health/funding-benchmarker

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: Why do most women's health tech pilots fail to scale?
A: 90% of health tech pilots fail to convert to full-scale implementation without clinical alignment (Accenture Health, Digital Health Scaling Report). The gap between a promising women's health platform and real-world clinical adoption isn't about the technology. It's about trust, workflow fit, and physician buy-in.

Q: How do you get physicians to adopt new health technology?
A: Physicians don't adopt technology because it's good. They adopt it because a trusted colleague told them it works. The Faulkner Group builds physician adoption through peer-to-peer advocacy, KOL engagement, CMIO engagement strategies, and internal champion development.

Q: What clinical governance frameworks do health systems require before approving new tech?
A: Health systems and payers require governance and risk frameworks including clinical environment audits, integration barrier identification, controlled rollback protocols, escalation pathways, and compliance documentation.

Q: What is The 4 Pillars of Clinical Adoption framework?
A: The four pillars are: (1) Clinical Readiness and Governance, (2) Physician Adoption and Champion Development, (3) Training, Education, and Change Management, (4) Sales, Customer Success, and Deployment.

Q: How do VC and PE firms reduce clinical adoption risk in health tech portfolios?
A: Through clinical and operational diligence that evaluates physician sentiment, governance readiness, EHR integration viability, and workflow alignment before a Series B, add-on acquisition, or board review.

Q: What is a Clinical Clarity Session and who is it for?
A: A Clinical Clarity Session is a 90-minute virtual diagnostic with a board-certified OB/GYN and a Healthcare IT Architect. It costs $1,500 and identifies the 1-3 highest-risk failure points. A written Clinical Clarity Summary is delivered within 48 hours.
